spans.anchor_text: the Company engage GHF
spans.quote_text: In the fourth quarter of 2015, Robert H. Eder (the Companys Chairman and Chief Executive Officer) and Frank K. Rogers (the Companys Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer) met with one of the Companys Class I rail partners ( Party A) to discuss joint commercial opportunities. During the meeting, Party A suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company to further develop strategic opportunities and enhance regional connectivity. Upon receiving a report of this meeting, in recognition of its public company obligations, the Board appointed a subcommittee of two independent directors, Frank W. Barrett and Richard W. Anderson, to interview potential investment bankers to represent the Company in possible negotiations with Party A and to assist the Board in evaluating additional strategic options for the realization of shareholder value, including potential change in control transactions. The subcommittee interviewed several investment banking firms, including Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC (the business of which was subsequently acquired by BMO Capital Markets Corp. and which we refer to as  GHF prior to such acquisition and  BMO thereafter). During the executive session of the Boards regular quarterly meeting held on January 27, 2016 (which Director Alfred P. Smith did not attend), the subcommittee recommended to the Board that the Company retain GHF as its investment banking firm. At that meeting, representatives of GHF discussed with the Board its qualifications and a proposed process should the directors ultimately decide to proceed with a change in control transaction. As part of the interview process, GHF represented to the Company that it would not represent any potential buyer of the Company in connection with an offer to purchase the Company or any person proposing to provide financing in connection with such an offer. Following this presentation, the Board approved the subcommittees recommendation and the Company engaged GHF. In selecting GHF, the Board considered, among other things, the fact that GHF was a reputable investment banking firm with substantial experience advising companies in the rail sector and in providing strategic advisory services in general, GHF had recent experience providing strategic advisory services in connection with the sale of a short-line railroad and, in the Boards opinion, GHF would commit appropriate attention, resources and personnel to the process.
spans.quote_text_normalized: in the fourth quarter of 2015, robert h. eder (the company's chairman and chief executive officer) and frank k. rogers (the company's vice president and chief commercial would not represent any potential buyer of the company in connection with an offer to purchase the company or any person proposing to provide financing in connection with such an offer. following this presentation, the board approved the subcommittee's recommendation and the company engaged ghf. in selecting ghf, the board considered, among other things, the fact that ghf was a reputable investment banking firm with substantial experience advising companies in the rail sector and in providing strategic advisory services in general, ghf had recent experience providing strategic advisory services in connection with the sale of a short-line railroad and, in the board's opinion, ghf would commit appropriate attention, resources and personnel to the process.

spans.anchor_text: On Friday, August 12, 2016, Providence and Worcester Railroad Company ("P& W" or the "Company") entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Genesee & Wyoming Inc. ("G& W")
spans.quote_text: I am writing to you all today regarding a significant announcement about the future of our Company. On Friday, August 12, 2016, Providence and Worcester Railroad Company ("P& W" or the "Company") entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Genesee & Wyoming Inc. ("G& W"). Because P& W is a publicly traded company, I was unfortunately legally barred from speaking with you before the agreement was reached and publicly announced. Over the next few days, you will likely see more

events.summary: Party C executed a confidentiality agreement.

Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence after the Company declined to commit not to enter into an agreement with another bidder during Party D's requested 30-day diligence period.
events.drop_reason_text: The Company declined to commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during Party D's 30-day due diligence period.

date.raw_text: which deadline was subsequently postponed to May 19, 2016

events.summary: The non-binding indication of interest deadline was extended to May 19, 2016.
Party A expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company during a meeting about joint commercial opportunities.
The Board approved retaining GHF as the Company's investment banking firm.
The Board decided to proceed with a transaction strategy involving discussions with Party A and soliciting interest from other potential third parties regarding a change in control transaction.
Potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers executed confidentiality agreements with the Company.
notes: The filing states each of the potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers executed confidentiality agreements, but does not identify all signatories individually.

events.summary: Potential buyers were advised to submit non-binding indications of interest by May 10, 2016, later postponed to May 19, 2016.
The Transaction Committee excluded the two low bidders from the process before management presentations.
events.drop_reason_text: In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations.

events.summary: The Company received nine written indications of interest by the extended non-binding deadline period.
Potential buyers were instructed to submit non-binding LOIs by July 20, 2016 together with mark-ups of transaction documents.
Party C approached the Company's management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company.
The Company received six LOIs in response to the July 20, 2016 non-binding LOI deadline.
The Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00.

terms.raw_text: offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million)

events.whole_company_scope_note: LOIs were requested on the basis of purchase of 100% of the outstanding common stock.

events.summary: Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for $24.00 per share, subject to an expedited diligence review.

terms.raw_text: price of $24.00 per share (subject to an expedited diligence review)

events.summary: Party C submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for $19.30 per share, subject to a 30-day diligence period.
Party D submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for $21.00 per share, subject to a four-week diligence period.
Party E submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for $21.26 per share, subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation.
Party F submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for $19.20 per share, subject to a 30-day due diligence period.
Party C submitted an IOI to acquire the Company at $21.00 per share.

terms.raw_text: offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million)

events.summary: G& W submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for $21.15 per share, subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period.
The Transaction Committee decided to proceed only with G& W and Party B and informed the remaining bidders they were no longer involved in the process.
events.drop_reason_text: The Company should proceed with G& W and Party B because of the higher offers made by each of G& W and Party B relative to the other potential buyers.

events.summary: Party D and Party E both expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing their offers.
Party D submitted a revised LOI at $24.00 per share.

terms.raw_text: revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share

events.summary: Party E submitted a revised LOI at $23.81 per share, along with financing support from Party F.

terms.raw_text: revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share

events.summary: Party E withdrew its revised proposal, while confirming its original proposal of $21.26 per share.
events.drop_reason_text: Withdrew its revised proposal; text does not state a more specific reason.

events.summary: Party B indicated that it would not increase its price after being asked whether it would increase its offer price.
notes: Text supports refusal to raise price, not an explicit withdrawal from the process.
events.drop_reason_text: Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.

events.summary: G& W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for $25.00 per share in cash, excluding the previously proposed CVR, together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and voting agreement.
notes: LOI stated the offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.

events.summary: The Company and G& W executed the merger agreement shortly after the Board approval.
The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction before the opening of trading on NASDAQ.
notes: Public announcement of the signed transaction by the target.

exclusions.explanation: Party A's expressed interest was in acquiring equity in the Company and possible joint venture arrangements, not a whole-company acquisition proposal.
These blocks describe meetings, due diligence, document exchanges, and negotiation activity without an extractable event type requiring inclusion here.
The statement that one strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI does not identify the bidders, so no bidder-specific drop event can be emitted.
Negotiations over draft merger agreement terms, diligence activity, and G& W's indication of a possible intention to submit a revised LOI do not by themselves constitute a taxonomy event.
Board deliberations, fairness opinion discussions, regulatory comparison, and later review of BMO independence are not standalone taxonomy events in these blocks.

The statement that G& W was '$1.00 more per share' than Party B does not provide explicit economics for Party B's offer in these supplied blocks, so no Party B proposal event is emitted from this text alone.

unresolved_mentions: Nine IOIs were received, but not all submitting bidders are identified by name or alias.
One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI; identities not specified.
The chronology notes the possibility that GATX might be a potential buyer, but no actual participation as a bidder is described.
Two additional Class I railroads were approached to determine interest, but they are not identified.
another bidder ("Party D") in B017 is linked in roster, but the other unnamed bidder referenced in B017 is not separately identifiable from the roster.
five interested parties with whom management had met at the 2016 Connections Convention in B009 are not fully individually identified.
nine written indications of interest from potential buyers in B013 are not individually attributed to roster actors, except Party C's later disclosed IOI.
two additional Class I railroads in B009 are not linked to roster actors.
extraction_notes: Extraction limited to chunk-1 chronology blocks through B031.
No executed event included because merger agreement execution appears outside supplied chronology blocks, though cross-filing evidence references August 12, 2016.
No proposal event emitted for Party A because the text does not state a whole-company proposal with explicit economics.

No NDA events were emitted for individually named bidders from B007 because the filing does not specify which named parties among the contacted groups executed confidentiality agreements, except Party C in B016.
The aggregate six-LOI range in B017 is included in addition to bidder-specific LOI proposals because the filing expressly states both the aggregate and the individual bids.
Chunk covers late-stage August 2016 negotiations, Board approval, signing, and public announcement.

Prior-round context indicates an earlier informal final round; the August 12, 2016 G& W revised LOI is therefore flagged as occurring after final-round announcement and after final-round deadline based on supplied context.
No NDA, final-round, or additional proposal events are supported within these blocks beyond the August 12, 2016 G& W revised LOI.
Did not emit a separate target-side sale press release event because the text describes announcement of the signed transaction, which is captured as bid_press_release under the allowed taxonomy.
